summary: The test suite covers main policies (max-age, keep-latest, max-total-size) and modes (dry-run, execute) with acceptable scope, but has critical structural issues and weak edge-case coverage. Major problems: (1) cleanup.bats references FIXTURE="$BATS_TEST_DIRNAME/fixtures/basic.tsv" which is not provided, making tests non-runnable; (2) act_harness copies .actrc from PROJECT_ROOT, also not provided; (3) no tests for empty input, malformed TSV (missing columns, non-numeric values), invalid dates, zero/negative sizes, boundary conditions (artifacts exactly on age cutoff), or all-three-policies-combined scenarios; (4) assertions lack context (grep -q suppresses output, making failures hard to debug); (5) no error message validation beyond exit code. The act_harness approach of inlining fixtures is better, but cleanup.bats is brittle. Positive: reasonable test separation, some combined-policy testing, end-to-end workflow validation. This suite would catch gross failures but miss subtle bugs and edge cases—not trustworthy for production artifact retention logic where correctness is critical.
